iterations.strategy: Root cause: the prior plan used a logical path that reconstructs pure-row candidates from a uom posting index built on 32-bit words, forcing broad entry scans, rowid materialization, sorting, and expensive hash-map build/merge work before useful filtering. Strategy category A/C: reorder to filter sub.fy first, drive fact access through num_adsh_fk_hash postings for only relevant filings, and perform uom/value filtering plus (adsh,tag)->max aggregation on that reduced stream; this removes the heavy pure_rowids preprocessing and cuts wasted build work while preserving exact max-equality semantics.

Root cause: build_joins dominates because many index-probed fact rows are materialized and re-probed through extra hash/row-vector passes after join order selection. Strategy category A/C: keep the empirically best join order (sub[fy=2022] -> num_adsh postings, validated by sampling: 79.6ms vs 121.9ms/138.3ms alternatives) and eliminate wasted work by deduplicating sub adsh keys, aggregating max(value) per (adsh,tag) directly on the posting stream, and emitting eq-max candidates without a separate filtered_num materialization pass.

Root cause: the current plan still performs two full passes over the same adsh-driven posting stream (first to build (adsh,tag)->max, second to re-probe for n.value=max), so build_joins remains dominant from repeated random fact-column touches and extra hash-map merge/probe work. Strategy category A/C: keep the validated join order (sub fy filter then num_adsh posting probes) but fuse aggregation and eq-max emission into a single per-adsh traversal using thread-local tag states that track max and tie rows, removing the global max_map build/merge and the second posting pass while preserving exact MAX equality semantics.
